headline: CONFIRMATION. April 24 corrected baseline at 84.4 (Exemplary). HRW Feb 2026 documentation of Operation Metro Surge unlawful deaths (Renee Good, Alex Pretti shot by federal agents in Minneapolis-St. Paul) is FEDERAL-enforcement context within state borders, not state-government action. State EQU score at 3.8 (lowest dimension) appropriately reflects federal-context vulnerability. Amnesty April 2026 'stop predatory era' call is contextual. Federal context provides downward pressure but is not state-attributable per indexing rule (state scoring measures state-government action and capacity). No state-acute negative event in window. Composite 84.4 holds at near-band-boundary; volatility flag remains.

evidence.finding: Operation Metro Surge: thousands of armed federal agents; documented unlawful deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti shot by federal agents; campaign of intimidation against immigrants and observers. ATTRIBUTABLE TO FEDERAL ACTORS, not state government.
Calls for states to 'stop predatory era' — calls Minnesota among states with strong rights frameworks to resist federal action.

negativeSignals: Federal context within state borders provides downward-pressure on EQU (state's ability to protect residents from federal action). EQU at 3.8 already reflects this constraint.
positiveSignals: Amnesty positioning of MN as locus of expected state-level pushback against federal predatory-era action is contextual positive INT/ACC signal.
scoring_attribution_note: Per state-index methodology, scoring measures state-government action and capacity — federal-actor unlawful deaths are not state-attributable, but state-protection capacity is reflected in EQU subscore.
